item.title: Ed Bindels’s Apple Museum in Utrecht, Netherlands 摘要
item.description:
A new Apple museum has opened in Utrecht, Netherlands, located about 30–40 minutes south of Amsterdam. The museum features a notable rainbow wall of iMacs, which is described as incredible. The article references Juli Clover as the source for this information.
标签: Apple, museum, Utrecht, Netherlands, iMac, technology, design, history, exhibition, Europe

item.title: Premium: The Hater's Guide to OpenAI 摘要
item.description:
The article criticizes Sam Altman and OpenAI for alleged deceptive practices, including misleading revenue reporting and unrealistic financial commitments. It claims OpenAI inflates revenue figures through creative accounting, such as counting free tokens as sales, and questions media complicity in uncritically reporting claims like a $600 billion compute spending target by 2030. The author argues OpenAI's business model is unsustainable, with costs potentially exceeding revenue, and draws parallels to past failures like FTX.
标签: AI, business, ethics, media, finance, OpenAI, deception, reporting, startups, controversy

item.title: Kākāpō parrots 摘要
item.description:
Lenny shared a video clip from a longer podcast recording with Simon Willison, focusing on kākāpō parrots. The post includes an embedded video with a preview image and is tagged with 'kakapo'.
标签: kakapo, parrots, podcast, wildlife, video, social media, nature, conservation, birds, Lenny

item.title: ★ Let Us Learn to Show Our Friendship for a Man When He Is Alive and Not After He Is Dead 摘要
item.description:
A New Yorker profile investigates Sam Altman's trustworthiness, focusing on his 2023 OpenAI ouster and return. Key findings include colleagues describing him as having a 'sociopathic lack of concern for consequences' and Aaron Swartz warning he 'can never be trusted.' The article also details his ambiguous departure from Y Combinator, where Paul Graham reportedly said Altman had been 'lying to us all the time,' though Graham publicly denies firing him. It raises questions about Altman's hiring of Fidji Simo, who faced FTC fines at Instacart, and suggests internal power struggles at OpenAI.
标签: Sam Altman, OpenAI, trustworthiness, leadership, AI ethics, corporate governance, Y Combinator, investigative journalism, business ethics, power dynamics
